component_identification.content: Alien power sheets/cards, home system planet set per player, ship miniatures/tokens, destiny deck, encounter cards (attack/negotiate/reinforcement), cosmic deck effects, warp, hyperspace gate, and colony markers. Each alien species breaks core rules in a distinct way.

core_game_loop.content: On your encounter, draw destiny to find a target, launch ships through the gate, invite allies, then both main players choose encounter cards face down (usually Attack or Negotiate). Resolve totals and card effects; winners establish colonies. First player to reach five foreign colonies wins, often with shared victory through alliances.

detailed_rules.content: Turn starts with regroup (retrieve ship from warp) then destiny draw to determine opponent unless card effects modify. Offense commits up to four ships from one colony through gate; defense commits from targeted colony. Both sides may invite allies in order, and invited players decide whether to send ships/cards for potential rewards. Encounter cards are revealed simultaneously: Attack adds card value plus ships and modifiers; Negotiate vs Attack causes compensation from attacker to defender if attacker wins, or deal opportunity if negotiate beats negotiate. If both negotiate, players must strike a deal for colonies/cards/resources as allowed; failing to deal penalizes both with card losses. Cosmic cards and alien powers can interrupt almost every stage, creating stack-like timing disputes resolved by rule hierarchy. Many powers are mandatory, optional, or flare-enhanced—check card text precisely.

scoring_and_endgame.content: A player wins immediately upon having five foreign colonies (colonies on other players’ planets). Allies on a successful offensive/defensive side may also gain colonies and share victory. Some alien powers replace or alter win conditions.

beginner_strategy.content: Use alliances aggressively; even small ally rewards build tempo. Hand quality matters—do not burn your best attack cards in low-stakes fights. Track who is near five colonies and coordinate table pressure. Learn timing windows for your alien power so you do not miss mandatory triggers. Be open to deals when both negotiate; refusing reasonable trades often harms both players more than rivals.
